summary:
Pre-build QA review of telemetry-dashboard domain PASSED. 50 Squad Leads launched 250 workers across 10 premium models auditing: swarm-state.json schema, child-agents.jsonl format, stampede-monitor live counts, premium model policy, launch-proof contract, launcher metaswarm code paths, Depth Guard enforcement, stop/recovery behavior, sub-agent terminology, and collaboration bus integration.

KEY FINDINGS:
• swarm-state.json schema: PASS — all 35 required fields present with correct types
• child-agents.jsonl: PASS — format, events, depth, model fields all compliant
• Monitor live counts: PASS — correctly reads telemetry with launch_proof fallback
• Premium model policy: PARTIAL — consistent in commander+launcher but absent from SKILL.md
• Launch-proof contract: PASS — success/partial/failed conditions clearly documented
• Launcher metaswarm: PASS — creates commander panes, uses stampede-commander agent, caps at 5
• Depth Guard: PASS — workers are leaf nodes, tools enforced, max_depth=3
• Stop/recovery: PASS — orphan recovery, heartbeat stale check, dead pane detection all correct
• Terminology: NEEDS WORK — 'sub-agent' vs 'child agent' inconsistent across files; 'metaswarm' capitalization varies; 'Agent Pulse' not a named feature in code
• Collaboration bus: PASS — directory creation, protocol.json, all 5 ledgers functional

NON-BLOCKING ISSUES: (1) premium_model_pool missing from SKILL.md, (2) terminology drift sub-agent/child-agent, (3) Metaswarm/metaswarm capitalization, (4) SKILL.md lacks collab bus documentation, (5) Squad Lead lowercase in agent.md lines 54-55.

notes: 8/50 identified non-blocking terminology and documentation gaps
No structural defects found in telemetry pipeline
